---
title: "Dead APIs"
---

These were tried, failed, and recorded so the guess is never made twice. When
a material or mesh API fails on an unknown surface, consult this table before
guessing again.

| Tried | Why it fails | Use instead |
|---|---|---|
| `Material.GetBuiltinMaterial(...)` | does not exist | `new Material(Shader.Find("Standard"))` |
| `material.mainColor` | removed in Unity 5 | `material.color` |
| `new Material(null)` | ambiguous, compile error | `new Material(Shader.Find("Standard"))` |
| {{< unity-type LocalToParent >}} | removed in modern Entities | {{< unity-type Parent >}}, {{< unity-type LocalTransform >}}, {{< unity-type LocalToWorld >}} |
| `float4x4.ExtractLocalTransform()` | does not exist | `LocalTransform.FromMatrix(matrix)` |

::: {.failure title="The recovery rule"}
If a material or mesh creation fails twice on an unknown API, stop. Do not write
a third guess. Look up the correct API from first principles, then add the dead
one to this table so it dies once.
:::

## The correct material idioms

```{.right}
var mat = new UnityEngine.Material(UnityEngine.Shader.Find("Standard"));
mat.color = new UnityEngine.Color(1f, 0.5f, 0f);
```

```{.exec name="Load an existing material and assign it"}
var go = UnityEngine.GameObject.Find("Cube");
var mat = UnityEditor.AssetDatabase.LoadAssetAtPath<UnityEngine.Material>("Assets/Materials/MyMat.mat");
if (mat != null && go != null) go.GetComponent<UnityEngine.Renderer>().material = mat;
return mat != null ? "assigned" : "material not found";
```

## Why a one-liner beats hand-built geometry

The dead-API list is shorter than it would be because the codex reaches for
`CreatePrimitive` before building meshes by hand. The fewer APIs a mutation
touches, the fewer ways it has to be dead.
